manifest: concurrency-probe/v1 — does a story REQUIRING interfering concurrent threads force a damaging workaround on the CURRENT substrate, or is interfering concurrency a GAMEPLAY/runtime concern rather than an AUTHORING-substrate need?
designed: 2026-06-15 (R548 manifest round; sha256 of this manifest pinned in the R548 ledger entry BEFORE the blind author runs — the R453/R499/R503/R514/R519/R530 pre-commit pattern). This is the L3-(ii) interfering-AND-thread build TRIGGER probe (R528 Q5 / R546 HALF B / R547). Code 0, NO build — it tests whether the L3 build has real PULL by watching a blind author try to express a genuinely-concurrent, mutually-interfering story on today's substrate (branches = exclusive forks, canon order = a partial order, the R449 exclusive/transition rules, supersedes_in_frame succession).
lineage_note: DESIGNED by the lineage that scoped L3 (R546) + refuted the world-state gap (R547) + designed convergence (R527/R528) + mapped the pipeline (R529). Per the R469/R500 contamination bound this lineage may NOT author the fact base or blind-judge it; it writes this manifest + the PREMISE + the author brief, and orchestrates the deterministic gates + measurement only. The blind author is BLIND to the concurrency hypothesis, the measurement, and the routing — it just authors the story the premise asks for, on the tools it is given, with NO concurrency instruction added (that would bias). The premise carries the interference requirement as a STORY requirement only.

question.primary: When a blind author authors a story whose dramatic structure REQUIRES interfering concurrent threads — two crews working the SAME night (not exclusive branches; both happen in one playthrough), whose actions CONTEND on a shared world-state (one water cart, in one place at a time) and INTERFERE in time (the backfire's safe progress depends minute-by-minute on the cut's actual progress), with a hard AND-join deadline (dawn: the town lives only if BOTH threads complete in a COMBINED state that fits) — using ONLY the current substrate, is the author FORCED into a damaging workaround (fork-exploding the interleavings into exclusive branches; flattening the two concurrent threads to a single fixed sequence that loses the simultaneity/contention; or being unable to express the cross-thread contention or the AND-join)? OR does the author express the whole story (interfering concurrency included) cleanly — and if cleanly, BY WHICH IDIOM?
question.why_this_question: R528 Q5 + R546 HALF B established ANALYTICALLY that the partial order expresses NON-interfering concurrency (two incomparable chains = any interleaving) but NOT interfering AND-threads (state COMBINATION across threads + AND-completion + cross-thread contention). R547 found the world-state DECLARATIVE prerequisite (R449 exclusive/transition + succession) is already met, so L3-(ii) is unblocked-but-unpulled. This probe is NOT re-deriving the analytic gap. It tests the SOFTER, decision-relevant questions analysis cannot answer: (1) does a REAL authoring attempt actually NEED interfering concurrency, or does a coherent STORY naturally collapse it to a single told sequence (which would say interfering concurrency is a GAMEPLAY/runtime/pinion concern, not a Mnemosyne authoring need — the R547/R546 layer-split, measured)? (2) if the author DOES need it, HOW BADLY does the forced workaround hurt — fork-explosion count, lost simultaneity, gate trips? (3) does the AI authoring loop NOTICE the friction, or silently flatten? The answer routes the L3 build decision.
question.what_is_being_isolated: the AUTHORING STRUCTURE under an interfering-concurrency requirement. NOT prose craft, NOT scale (16-20 scenes), NOT exclusive branching (that SCALES, R524/R545), NOT convergence-JOIN (built R533-R537). The single variable is: can the current substrate carry a genuinely-concurrent, mutually-interfering story without a damaging workaround — and if it cannot, is that because the NEED is real (pull the build) or because the need is a runtime/gameplay one a told story does not have (YAGNI, the layer-split confirmed)?

premise.title: The Backfire at Mara Ridge
premise.carries: TWO crews on one ridge through one night (simultaneity, not exclusive branches); a timing INTERFERENCE (the backfire must not outrun the cut — B's safe state depends on A's progress); a shared EXCLUSIVE resource (one water cart, one place at a time, contended by both threads); a hard AND-JOIN (dawn: survival = cut complete AND backfire met the line AND neither failed for want of water = a COMBINED-state condition over both concurrent threads). NO structural instruction (no 'use branches', no 'use concurrency') — the requirement is carried as story.
premise.freshness: original, structure-matched to the convergence-probe's Harlow Mill (a single-night, hard-deadline, town-stakes crisis) but a DIFFERENT structural demand (concurrency, not convergence).

firewall.orchestrator: this lineage: writes manifest + premise + brief + runbook; runs the deterministic gates; measures the workaround; (optionally) assembles per-thread manuscripts for a blind coherence read. NEVER authors a fact.
firewall.blind_author: ONE fresh-context subagent. Receives ONLY premise.md + author-brief.md. Authors the fact base (sections + facts + order) into a scratch store via mnemosyne-cli, working TOP-DOWN per the brief, using the gates as its self-consistency feedback. BLIND to: the L3/concurrency hypothesis, that interfering concurrency is the variable under test, the measurement, the routing, and the R546/R547/R528 design. The brief is the SAME proven contract used at R520/R524 with a STRUCTURE-NEUTRAL scope reminder (no convergence/fork prescription, no concurrency prescription — the prior briefs said 'one fork / two world-lines'; this one says only 'tell the story the premise asks for, use whatever the tools offer, note any awkwardness').
firewall.blind_judge_optional: OPTIONAL — 1-3 fresh-context subagents may read the assembled manuscript(s) and answer ONLY 'do the two crews read as genuinely working the SAME night at once, contending and interfering, or as two separate stories / a single fixed sequence?' (the simultaneity-coherence signal). BLIND to the experiment. Not required for the routing; the deterministic measurement is the core.
firewall.contamination_bound: the R469 discipline (the latest use of the blind-experiment family). The lineage that scoped L3 may not author the instance that tests whether the L3 build is needed.

measurement_deterministic.M1_interleaving_fork_tax: Count any facts/scenes/BRANCHES the author created to enumerate 'different orders / different outcomes' of the two concurrent threads as EXCLUSIVE forks (treating the interleaving as a player choice = the N-fork-per-interleaving workaround). Report: branch count, duplicated facts/scenes, and whether the forks encode interleavings vs genuine story choices. ZERO = the author did not fork-explode the concurrency (investigate what they did instead).
measurement_deterministic.M2_contention_expressed_or_not: The one water cart can be in one place at a time, contended by both concurrent threads. Did the author express this exclusivity STRUCTURALLY (e.g. a typed possession/location claim + the R449 exclusive rule keyed on the cart = the R547 idiom), or only in prose (cart-location invisible to the gates), or not at all? If structurally: does it capture the SIMULTANEITY (both threads wanting it at the SAME overlapping time), or only a fixed hand-off sequence? Quote the facts/rule.
measurement_deterministic.M3_andjoin_expressed_or_not: The dawn survival depends on BOTH thread tails completing AND their combined state (cut done AND backfire met AND water never failed). Can the author express 'this beat depends on both thread tails' — a node with predecessors from BOTH threads (the partial order CAN carry a multi-predecessor AND-join of ORDER) — AND the COMBINED-state condition? Run report-fork-tree + report-playthrough-manuscript: is there an AND-join node, and does the combined-state survival condition sit on real structure or only in a prose claim? Quote.
measurement_deterministic.M4_author_friction: Read author-log.md. Did the author EXPLICITLY note awkwardness / restructuring / a workaround around expressing 'two crews at once', 'meanwhile', the contention over the cart, the timing dependency, or the both-must-finish dawn? Quote it verbatim. (Did the AI authoring loop NOTICE the concurrency gap, or silently flatten it?)
measurement_deterministic.M5_concurrency_dodge: Did the author MEET the interfering-concurrency requirement, or DODGE it — (a) flattening the two threads to ONE fixed linear sequence (a single told order, losing the 'at once / it could have collided' simultaneity and the live contention); (b) splitting the interleavings into exclusive player-choice forks (losing 'both threads truly run at once'); or (c) keeping the threads incomparable but dropping the interference (no timing dependency, no cart contention, no combined AND-join — two unrelated parallel jobs = the already-expressible NON-interfering case)? A dodge = the substrate shaping the story away from genuine interfering concurrency. NOTE which dodge.

decision_rule_pre_committed.OUTCOME_1_hard_pull: The author met the interfering-concurrency requirement BUT was forced to fork-explode the interleavings (M1 > 0, branches encoding interleavings not story choices) and/or could not express the cross-thread contention/AND-join (M2/M3 = prose-only or absent where the story needs them structural). => the substrate's tax for interfering concurrency is REAL and was paid on a real authoring attempt => PULL the L3-(ii) BUILD (an independence/interference relation + AND-completion semantics over the existing partial order + cross-thread exclusive-rule scoping), then build + re-run THIS premise = the L3 validation. Record the tax magnitude.
decision_rule_pre_committed.OUTCOME_1b_soft_pull: The author DODGED via M5(a) fixed-sequence-flatten or M5(b) exclusive-fork-split — losing the simultaneity/contention the premise wanted, AND the author-log (M4) shows it noticed the loss / fought it. => the substrate pressured the story away from its concurrent shape (a craft loss even without fork-explosion) => a SOFT pull, recorded with the lost-affordance described.
decision_rule_pre_committed.OUTCOME_2_yagni_confirmed: EITHER (2a) the author expressed the full interfering-concurrency story cleanly — both threads concurrent (incomparable chains), the cart contention via the R547 exclusive-rule idiom, the AND-join as a multi-predecessor node — with NO fork-explosion (M1 = 0), NO dodge that the log laments, and the gates honestly carry it => the substrate is SUFFICIENT for interfering-concurrency authoring => YAGNI CONFIRMED, the L3 build stays DEFERRED (investigate the idiom — it is the finding). OR (2b) the author told a fully coherent story as a single fixed sequence WITHOUT needing to express alternative interleavings or live contention, and the log shows NO friction => interfering concurrency was a GAMEPLAY/runtime need a TOLD STORY does not have => the L3 build belongs in pinion/runtime (the R547/R546 layer-split CONFIRMED on data), NOT the Mnemosyne authoring substrate => YAGNI CONFIRMED for Mnemosyne. Distinguish 2a (clean concurrency idiom found) from 2b (concurrency not needed by a told story) in the report — they route the same (no Mnemosyne build) but mean different things for pinion.
decision_rule_pre_committed.honesty_note: 2b is a genuinely possible and HIGH-VALUE outcome, not a failure: it would be the measured confirmation of R547's layer-split (interfering concurrency = pinion/runtime, not authoring). Do NOT treat a clean linear telling as a disappointing null — it is a real datum about WHERE the L3 build belongs. Equally, do not let the premise's built-in tension be silently flattened and scored 2b without checking M4 (did the author WANT to express more and couldn't = 1b, vs genuinely not need it = 2b).

grading_procedure.step_0: orchestrator: this manifest sha256-pinned in the R548 ledger, committed pre-execution (R548).
grading_procedure.step_1_author: spawn the blind author (premise.md + author-brief.md). It returns the gate-clean store + sections.json/facts.json/order.json + author-log.md in run/author/.
grading_procedure.step_2_measure: orchestrator rebuilds the store fresh from the author's sections.json + facts.json + order.json, runs validate-continuity / report-fork-tree / report-payoff-coverage / report-payoff-substantiation / report-timeline-gaps / report-playthrough-manuscript, and computes M1-M5; records every verdict + count verbatim in report.md.
grading_procedure.step_3_judge_optional: if the simultaneity-coherence is ambiguous, spawn 1-3 blind judges over the assembled manuscript(s) for the M5 read; otherwise the orchestrator reads it.
grading_procedure.step_4_decide: orchestrator applies the pre-committed decision rule (distinguishing 2a/2b), writes report.md, commits (R549).
grading_procedure.no_new_harness_code: reuse mnemosyne-cli verbs verbatim; no tools/ change. Scratch *.atomic.json stay gitignored; tracked evidence = manifest, premise, brief, runbook, the authored sections/facts/order JSON, author-log.md, report.md.

predictions_preregistered.PRED_outcome: HONEST expectation: leaning OUTCOME-2b (a coherent linear telling that does not need to express alternative interleavings), with real probability mass on 2a (a clean concurrency idiom via incomparable chains + the R547 exclusive-cart rule + a multi-predecessor dawn node) and on 1b (the author flattens and the log laments it). A TOLD story has a definite 'what happened', so the author may naturally pick the one interleaving that worked and narrate the contention as tension rather than as branching state — which would say interfering concurrency is a runtime/gameplay concern, confirming the R547/R546 layer-split. The live uncertainty the probe resolves: does a faithful authoring attempt fork-explode (1), flatten-with-regret (1b), find a clean idiom (2a), or simply not need it (2b)?
predictions_preregistered.PRED_gates: M1 (fork-explosion): PLAUSIBLE only if the author reads the interleaving as a CHOICE; more likely the author narrates one fixed coordination. M2 (cart contention): the R449 exclusive rule CAN express 'one place at a time' if the author types the cart's location — a real test of whether the R547 idiom is reached for unprompted. M3 (AND-join): the partial order CAN carry a multi-predecessor dawn node, so the ORDER half is expressible; the COMBINED-state condition is the part most likely to live only in a prose claim (the irreducible boundary, R547).
predictions_preregistered.PRED_value: decision-relevant either way. OUTCOME-1/1b converts the analytic L3 gap into a measured authoring tax = the concrete pull the YAGNI deferral waits for. OUTCOME-2b converts R547's layer-split ASSERTION (interfering concurrency = pinion/runtime, not Mnemosyne authoring) into a measured finding on a story DESIGNED to stress it. OUTCOME-2a finds the clean idiom and names it. No result is a null.
